concept: Selfish / Concerned Only with Self / Lacking Generosity
description: Lacking consideration for others; concerned chiefly with one's own personal profit or pleasure. 'Kaesu' names the quality of selfishness—the contracted heart that takes without giving.
sound_symbolism: The word grasps inward. 'Ka' begins with sharp claiming, the stop seizing what it touches, the open 'a' a hand opened only to grab. 'Su' sustains the self-focus, the hiss of the fricative circling back to the deep 'u' of the hoard. Then 'no' closes the door: the nasal turned away from others, the round 'o' sealing the circle shut. The vowels narrow from open 'a' through inward 'u' to closed-off 'o'—a movement of contraction, the shape of a heart that takes without giving.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'kasuno miona' (selfish person). Opposite of 'heloi' (generous). Names state that harms community and self.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Selfishness arises from illusion of separate self—seeing through this illusion naturally opens to generosity.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Selfish communication takes without giving—monologue that doesn't listen, speech that doesn't care for impact.
